home.title: Karyl Chan
home.lead: Public surfaces will live here in the future.

systemMsg.pin: {name} pinned a message to this channel.
systemMsg.boostTier: {name} just boosted the server — it's now Tier {tier}!

messageMgmt.bulkCountLabel: Number of recent messages to delete (2..100)

roleMgmt.fieldPermissionsAdvanced: Permission bitfield (kept in sync with the checkboxes)
roleMgmt.advancedHint: Pasting a number flips the checkboxes; ticking checkboxes updates the number.

profile.title: Profile
profile.hint: DM the bot with "login" again to refresh your session.

animatedEmoji.label: Autoplay animated emoji
animatedEmoji.help: Off shows a static frame for animated emoji — useful on mobile data.

desktopNotifications.label: Desktop notifications
desktopNotifications.help: Pop a system notification for new DMs and server {'@'}mentions when this window isn't focused. Muted channels don't notify.

auth.title: Sign in
auth.noTokenLead: To sign in, send a direct message to the bot:
auth.noTokenHint: The bot will reply with a single-use login link. Open the link in this browser.
auth.errorHint: Request a new link by sending {login} to the bot.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noPermission: No permission to view this section.

dmChart.title: DM activity
dmChart.subtitle: past 7 days
dmChart.ariaLabel: Bar chart showing DM activity over the past 7 days

attention.title: Needs attention
attention.dbDisconnectedDesc: The bot cannot persist data. Check your database connection.

settings.name: Server name

verification.3: High — member of this server for >10 minutes

settings.mfaOwnerOnly: Only the server owner can change this.

invites.title: Invite links
invites.fieldChannelHint: Where the invite drops people in. Defaults to the system channel.
invites.fieldTemporaryHint: Members get kicked when they disconnect unless they're assigned a role first.
invites.fieldUniqueHint: When off, Discord reuses an equivalent existing invite if one exists.

feature.todoHint: Messages in these channels become tracked todo items.
feature.rconHint: Forward messages to a Source-engine RCON server.
feature.roleEmojiGroupsHint: Buckets of emoji→role mappings. A watched message can pin specific groups, so the same emoji can grant different roles depending on the message.
feature.roleEmojiCreateGroupFirst: Create an emoji group above before adding mappings.
feature.roleEmojiHint: When a member reacts with the emoji on a watched message, they receive the role. Each mapping lives inside one group.
feature.roleReceiveHint: Watch these messages for role-emoji reactions. Each watched message is bound to one emoji group; reactions only resolve to a role when the emoji belongs to that group.
feature.capabilityHint: Grant a per-server capability to a role, so members of that role can use a slash command.

automod.title: AutoMod rules
automod.fieldTriggerImmutable: Trigger type can't be changed after creation.
automod.fieldKeywordsHint: Up to 1,000 entries; * matches any chars at word boundaries.
automod.needsAction: At least one action is required

messages.selectChat: Select a chat to view messages.
messages.noDms: No DMs yet. Send the bot a message in Discord, or start one above.

accessDenied.title: Access denied
accessDenied.body: Your role doesn't carry the admin capability. Ask an existing admin to grant it.

users.title: Authorized users
users.empty: Only the bot owner is authorized.
users.ownerNote: The bot owner is always authorized with every capability — no entry needed.
users.noMatches: No users match the current filters.

roles.title: Roles
roles.noCapabilities: No capabilities — users with this role will be unauthorized.

capabilityTabs.globalHint: Apply to every server and surface the bot reaches.
capabilityTabs.perGuildHint: Restrict the same scopes to a single server — these add to global tokens, they don't replace them.
capabilityTabs.perBehaviorTabHint: Grant the role CRUD over behaviors within specific scope tabs only. Adding/removing tabs themselves still requires behavior.manage.
capabilityTabs.perPluginHint: Capabilities each enabled plugin declared for its own use (e.g. WebUI access). These are removed automatically when a plugin is deleted or re-registered without them.

roles.noBehaviorTabs: No behavior scope tabs match this filter.
roles.legacyBehaviorDesc: Legacy token — revoke and replace with a per-tab grant above.
roles.noPluginCapabilities: No enabled plugin declares any capabilities.
roles.noPluginCapabilitiesFiltered: No plugin capabilities match this filter.
roles.orphanedPluginDesc: plugin disabled or capability removed — revoke if no longer needed

capabilityDesc.admin: Full access — bypasses every other capability check
capabilityDesc.dm.message: Read + write DM channels, messages, reactions, and unread counts
capabilityDesc.guild.message: Read + write messages and reactions in every server's channels
capabilityDesc.guild.manage: Manage members, roles, settings, and bot features in every server
capabilityDesc.system.read: View system event log and runtime stats
capabilityDesc.behavior.manage: Manage all targets and rules of the webhook behavior module

guildScoped.message: Read + write messages and reactions in this server only.
guildScoped.manage: Manage members, roles, settings, and bot features in this server only.

systemSettings.title: System settings
systemSettings.subtitle: Environment-variable configuration frozen at boot — read-only.

runtime.title: Runtime-editable settings
runtime.empty: All settings are frozen at boot — no runtime modifications available

jwtKey.title: JWT signing key
jwtKey.desc: The Ed25519 key the bot signs every JWT with (admin login links, plugin WebUI session tokens). Generated at runtime, stored encrypted in the database; the public key is published to plugins.
jwtKey.ephemeral: Running on an ephemeral in-memory key — ENCRYPTION_KEY isn't set, so the key can't be persisted and rotation is unavailable. Tokens won't survive a bot restart.
jwtKey.rotateConfirmMessage: Generates a fresh key and immediately invalidates every outstanding token — admin login links and plugin WebUI sessions. Plugins pick up the new public key within ~30 s; affected users will need to re-authenticate.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Sibling services registered with the bot. A plugin self-registers on startup; once enabled it can receive events and be referenced by behaviors.
plugins.emptyHint: Plugins auto-register at startup. Make sure the container is on the same docker network and the per-plugin secret is provisioned — use the Add plugin button above for an easy start, or run POST /api/plugins/setup-secret manually.

addPlugin.title: Add a plugin
addPlugin.body: Enter the plugin's key (its manifest `id`). The bot creates a placeholder entry and gives you a one-time setup secret to put in the plugin's `.env` (`KARYL_PLUGIN_SETUP_SECRET`) before you start it.
addPlugin.alreadyExists: This key already had an entry — its setup secret was regenerated (the old one is now invalid).

scopes.pendingHint: {n} new RPC scope(s) awaiting admin approval — click to review
scopes.approveConfirm: The following RPC scopes are requested by "{name}". Once approved they take effect immediately:

setupSecret.confirmBody: A new setup secret will be generated for "{name}". The old secret will be invalidated immediately — you must write the new value into this plugin's .env and restart it. Continue?
setupSecret.instruction: Copy the value below into your plugin .env and restart the plugin:
setupSecret.warning: This value will not be shown again. If lost, you must regenerate (which invalidates the current one).
setupSecret.checkboxLabel: I have saved this secret to the plugin .env

plugins.deleteConfirm: Delete offline plugin "{name}"? This will also remove all its KV data, config, and feature settings and cannot be undone.
plugins.cannotDeleteActive: Cannot delete an active plugin — stop the plugin and wait for it to go inactive first
plugins.deleteAllConfirm: Delete all {n} offline plugins? Each plugin's KV, config, and feature settings will be removed and cannot be recovered.

behaviors.intro: The behaviors below are declared by this plugin. You can enable or disable each behavior here; disabled behaviors will not be registered as Discord slash commands by the reconciler.

commands.intro: The slash commands below are declared by this plugin. Axes are locked by the plugin manifest — admin can only enable or disable individual commands.
commands.empty: This plugin declares no custom commands

features.intro: The features below can be toggled per-server on the Bot Functions tab of each guild page. This tab is read-only.
features.empty: This plugin provides no guild features

scopes.approveConfirm: Approve the following RPC scopes requested by "{name}"?

page.pickTarget: Pick a target from the sidebar.

sidebar.title: Behaviors
sidebar.globalAllHint: Applies to all platforms and contexts

addPlugin.title: Add plugin behavior
addPlugin.noEligible: No eligible plugins. Enable a plugin that exposes a dm_behavior on the Plugins page first.

workspace.deleteTabConfirm: Delete tab "{label}"? All behaviors in this tab will also be removed.

card.title: Title
card.regexInvalid: Trigger value is not a valid regex.
card.sourceNoticPlugin: This behavior is provided by Plugin "{name}". Some fields are read-only.
card.sourceNoticSystem: System built-in behavior. The trigger type and its values can be edited; other fields are locked.

addModal.title: Add Behavior
addModal.subtitle: Create an operator-defined behavior: pick a trigger, then choose where it's forwarded.

allServers.botFeaturesDesc: Set each guild feature's default on/off state. The default applies to every server that hasn't been given an explicit override on its own page — change it here and it takes effect everywhere immediately (no extra step). A server with an override keeps that override until you clear it on the server page.
allServers.noPluginFeatures: No plugins have declared any guild features.

voice.title: Voice Playback
voice.description: When enabled, guild members can use the bot's voice features in voice channels (e.g. /voice commands or plugin playback controls).
voice.cmdJoin: Make the bot join your current voice channel

pluginSecurity.setupSecretDesc: Regenerating the Setup Secret immediately invalidates the old one. The plugin must re-register with the bot using the new secret.
pluginSecurity.rpcScopesDesc: The plugin declares the RPC methods it wants to call in its manifest; only the scopes you approve are written into its token. An unapproved scope is rejected even when declared.
pluginSecurity.rpcScopesNone: This plugin declares no RPC scopes.
